title: eriophorum bellardii
intro:
Eriophorum angustifolium, not Eriophorum bellardii, seems to be the plant you are looking for. However, I couldn't find information about Eriophorum bellardii. 

Eriophorum angustifolium, also known as cotton-grass or tussock-cotton-grass, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is a wildflower that grows in wetland areas and produces white, cotton-like seed heads. 

The medicinal properties of Eriophorum angustifolium include its use as a diuretic and expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Its root and leaves are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The health benefits of Eriophorum angustifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. It has also been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.

The herbal preparations of Eriophorum angustifolium include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from its roots, leaves, and flowers. These preparations can be used internally to treat respiratory issues and externally to soothe skin conditions.
